verdict_reasoning: ITPR1 is a multi-pass transmembrane channel in the ER membrane that releases Ca2+ from ER stores upon IP3 binding; its bulk cytosolic regulatory/ligand-binding domain faces the cytoplasm and its TM segments are embedded in the ER, not the plasma membrane. Walking the contextual buckets: no documented cancer/cell-death/activation-induced PM outer-leaflet display of ITPR1 itself; no narrow germline/developmental somatic-lineage surface display; not a lysosomal TM protein routed via lysosomal exocytosis; no documented PM minority pool or secretory cycling to the surface (ER-PM junctional contacts keep it on the ER face); no wash-resistant covalent/non-covalent tethering to a TM partner on the outer leaflet.
